headline: Adobe (ADBE) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
summary: Over the coming months, I will be working with Frank Calderoni, Adobe's Lead Director and the Board of Directors to identify my successor and to ensure a smooth transition.  Until then, I will continue to lead Adobe as CEO and will stay on as Chair of the Board to support my successor just as John and Chuck did when I took on this role.  What attracted me to Adobe 28 years ago remains unchanged: our leadership in creating new market categories, world-class products, a relentless drive to innovate in every functional area of the company and our employees who continue to invent the future.

content.title: Adobe (ADBE) Q1 2026 Earnings Call Transcript
content.summary: Over the coming months, I will be working with Frank Calderoni, Adobe's Lead Director and the Board of Directors to identify my successor and to ensure a smooth transition.  Until then, I will continue to lead Adobe as CEO and will stay on as Chair of the Board to support my successor just as John and Chuck did when I took on this role.  What attracted me to Adobe 28 years ago remains unchanged: our leadership in creating new market categories, world-class products, a relentless drive to innovate in every functional area of the company and our employees who continue to invent the future.

headline: Adobe CEO Exit Puts AI Growth And Valuation In Investor Focus
summary: Adobe (NasdaqGS:ADBE) announced that CEO Shantanu Narayen will step down after a successor is appointed, ending an 18 year tenure. The leadership update came alongside Q1 results that the company described as strong, with 12% revenue growth. Adobe also reported that its AI first annualized recurring revenue has tripled, signaling rapid uptake of new AI offerings. Adobe is best known for its Creative Cloud and Document Cloud franchises, which sit at the center of content creation, marketing...

headline: ADBE Q1 Deep Dive: AI Transition, CEO Succession, and Freemium Push Shape Outlook
summary: Creative software giant Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 12% year on year to $6.40 billion. Guidance for next quarter’s revenue was better than expected at $6.46 billion at the midpoint, 0.5% above analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $6.06 per share was 3.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: ADBE Q1 Deep Dive: AI Transition, CEO Succession, and Freemium Push Shape Outlook
content.summary: Creative software giant Adobe (NASDAQ:ADBE) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 12% year on year to $6.40 billion. Guidance for next quarter’s revenue was better than expected at $6.46 billion at the midpoint, 0.5% above analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $6.06 per share was 3.2% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Adobe shares drop after CEO exit adds to AI-disruption concerns
summary: Adobe's shares plunged 9% in premarket trading on Friday after the Photoshop maker said CEO Shantanu ‌Narayen would step down after 18 years at the helm, ‌unsettling investors already wary of AI-driven disruptions to the design software market.  The longtime CEO's exit ​comes at a critical juncture as Adobe works to reassure investors it can keep pace with sweeping changes brought by artificial intelligence in the software landscape.  It follows a broader slide in software stocks after fears that ‌AI agents could supplant ⁠some traditional applications that led to a nearly $1 trillion rout in software stocks globally last month.

content.title: Adobe shares drop after CEO exit adds to AI-disruption concerns
content.summary: Adobe's shares plunged 9% in premarket trading on Friday after the Photoshop maker said CEO Shantanu ‌Narayen would step down after 18 years at the helm, ‌unsettling investors already wary of AI-driven disruptions to the design software market.  The longtime CEO's exit ​comes at a critical juncture as Adobe works to reassure investors it can keep pace with sweeping changes brought by artificial intelligence in the software landscape.  It follows a broader slide in software stocks after fears that ‌AI agents could supplant ⁠some traditional applications that led to a nearly $1 trillion rout in software stocks globally last month.
